14,000 张法国大革命图片在线发布（2014 年）

14,000 Images of the French Revolution Released Online (2014)

hyperallergic.com 作者 downbad_ 分数 21 评论 1 2026/04/12 01:15
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 艺术 斯坦福大学和法国国家图书馆合作建立的法国大革命数字档案馆于本周宣布推出，其中包含约 14,000 张高分辨率图像。
  2. 皇冠：“我失去了头”；断头台：“我找到了一个”（1793）（来自法国大革命数字档案馆）
  3. 断头台上被推倒的王冠和滚筒只是本月在线发布的大量图像和文件档案的一部分。斯坦福大学和法国国家图书馆合作建立的法国大革命数字档案馆于上周宣布推出，其中包含约 14,000 张高分辨率图像。对于任何试图使用详尽但繁琐的 BnF 网站进行研究的人来说，这是一个令人高兴的消息。然而，即使对于好奇的非学术界人士来说，该档案也非常用户友好，并且在历史上最动荡的时代之一提供了丰富的生动材料。该档案是一个多年项目，分为两个区域——议会档案和法国大革命图像——每次搜索都包含一个非常方便的视觉时间轴，这样您就可以准确地确定您想要的目标时间点。虽然这些图像充满了印刷品和插图，但也有奖章、硬币和其他文物的照片。你可以通过“宗教”、可悲的“国王和王室”以及混乱的“政治对抗和社会冲突”等主题来处理这些图像。艺术和文化也有区分，比如“英雄”和“寓言”，甚至还有令人兴奋的“闺阁政治”。当你查看图像时，关键词是法语，但总的来说，英语或法语国家都可以使用。以下只是档案中的一些发现，其中有革命恶棍和英雄的漫画、关键事件的插图，当然还有给数以万计的公民带来致命剃须的“国家剃刀”的崛起：断头台。
  4. 孔代亲王路易·约瑟夫 (190-92) 的漫画（来自法国大革命数字档案馆）
  5. 给国王上“课”的讽刺图片（1791）（来自法国大革命数字档案馆）

Original Summary

  1. Art The French Revolution Digital Archive, a partnership between Stanford University and the Bibliothèque nationale de France, was announced this week with some 14,000 high-resolution images.
  2. Crown: “I lost a head”; Guillotine: “I’ve found one” (1793) (via French Revolution Digital Archive)
  3. Toppled crowns and tumbrels to the guillotine are just part of the massive archive of images and documents released online this month. The French Revolution Digital Archive, a partnership between Stanford University and the Bibliothèque nationale de France, was announced last week with some 14,000 high-resolution images.For anyone who tried to use the thorough, but cumbersome, BnF website for research, this is joyous news. Yet even for curious non-academics the archive is incredibly user-friendly and rich with vivid material on one of history’s most tumultuous times. The archive, a multi-year project, is divided into two areas — Parliamentary Archives and Images of the French Revolution — with a very handy visual timeline included on each search so you can pinpoint what exact point of time you want to target. While the images are heavy on prints and illustrations, there are also photographs of medals, coins, and other artifacts.You can approach the images by themes like “religion,” the woeful “King and the Royal Family,” and the chaotic “political rivalries and social conflict.” There are also divisions in art and culture, like “heroes” and “allegories,” and even the titillating “politics in the boudoir.” The keywords once you’re viewing an image are in French, but overall it’s accessible to either anglo- or francophones.Below are just a few finds from the archive, with caricatures of revolutionary villains and heroes, illustrations of pivotal events, and of course the rise of the “National Razor” that gave tens of thousands of citizens a deadly shave: the guillotine.
  4. Caricature of Louis-Joseph, Prince of Condé (190-92) (via French Revolution Digital Archive)
  5. A satiric image of a “lesson” being given to the king (1791) (via French Revolution Digital Archive)
